title: Tool Manifest
description: Declares everything the engine needs to know about a tool: its identity, its inputs, its render target, and its capabilities. The template consumes inputs by name; this manifest declares them.

id.description: Stable, lowercase, hyphen-separated. NEVER changes once published. URL-safe.

a11yLabel.description: Optional Handlebars template for the canvas's accessible label (the preview is exposed to screen readers as role=img). Hydrated live with input values, e.g. "QR code linking to {{url}}". Defaults to "<name> preview" when omitted.

version.description: SemVer. Bumped on any change. Used for cache-busting and saved-state migration.

engineVersion.description: SemVer range of engine versions this tool is compatible with, e.g. '^1.0.0'.

status.description: Drives gallery sorting, watermarking, and brand-approval messaging. Experimental tools watermark their exports.

category.description: Free-form, used for gallery grouping. Examples: 'everyone', 'event', 'product', 'designer'.

privacy.description: Marks an on-device utility — a tool that processes the user's OWN content (file in → transformed file out) entirely on the device, with nothing uploaded. The host shows a 'Runs on your device — nothing is uploaded' badge. Enforced conventions (validated): status must not be 'experimental' (no watermark on the user's own file) and the tool must not embed provenance metadata into the output. This is the marker for the privacy-flagship utility category (EXIF strip, redact, compress, convert) that replaces content exfiltration to single-purpose SaaS.

render.description: Defines the template render area and supported export formats.

formats.description: Output formats the tool supports. The host filters action buttons to these. Clipboard is an action (see render.actions), not a format.

actions.description: Default actions to show. Defaults to ['copy', 'download', 'save'] if omitted.

export.description: Set false for utility/interactive tools that have no export. Hides download, copy, format, and dimension controls. Shows Save only when the tool has inputs. Hides the sidebar entirely when there are no inputs.

layout.description: Presentation of the tool's interface. 'sidebar' (default) renders inputs in the left control panel. 'canvas' hides the sidebar and presents the tool as a full-bleed working area — a single declared 'file' input is shown as a drag-and-drop / click-to-pick zone on the canvas itself (for on-device file utilities). 'editor' hides the sidebar too, but keeps the fixed-size render canvas AND the full render/export controls (render pill + export popup): a chromeless WYSIWYG surface. A tool using 'editor' declares one 'blocks' input carrying a `canvas` flag (which maps its geometry fields); the web shell mounts a direct-manipulation overlay (select / drag / resize / rotate / z-order / align / distribute) that reads and writes that flat blocks array. Data stays flat and URL-expressible, so CLI/URL mode render the same artwork with no overlay. Input semantics are unchanged.

dims.description: Set false to hide the export dimension inputs in the download bar.

printMarks.description: Set false to hide the 'Print marks & bleed' card from the download bar. The print-finishing path (crop/registration marks, bleed box, page boxes) applies to a single trim-sized artwork, so multi-page documents (which emit per-page boxes) opt out of it.

transparentBg.description: Adds a 'No BG' (transparent background) toggle to the export bar. The engine injects it into the model so hooks can react to it via onInit/onInput.

liveMaxEdge.description: For onFrame (live camera) tools only: the requested longest edge, in pixels, of the working camera frame handed to the hook. The shell downscales the source camera to a small default that suits a vector trace; a raster-output tool (whose result is a bitmap, not traced shapes — e.g. pixel-stretch) sets this higher for sharper output. The shell clamps it to the native camera frame (never upscales) and to its own ceiling. Ignored by tools without onFrame and by shells without a camera.

convertPaths.description: Controls the 'Convert paths' export toggle (outline text to vector paths in SVG/PDF/PDF-CMYK so output renders identically without the fonts installed). The engine auto-injects the toggle whenever the tool exports a vector format. Set false to suppress the toggle entirely and never outline text — e.g. capture tools, where the output is a raster and text-outlining doesn't apply.

preview.description: Marks a tool whose live canvas is a placeholder until an explicit, expensive render runs (e.g. a capture tool that screenshots a page in beforeExport). The shell wires a [data-preview] control in the template to render into the frame — preview only, never a download — and re-shows the placeholder whenever an input changes (the last preview is invalidated).

format.description: Format used for the preview render. Defaults to the tool's first render format. Pick a cheap raster (e.g. webp) — the blob is discarded; only the painted frame is kept.

auto.description: Generate a preview automatically once the tool finishes loading, so the user lands on a rendered frame rather than the placeholder. Fires once on load only — never on every input change — so deferred renders stay deliberate.

video.description: Default video capture settings used when webm or mp4 is in formats.

wait.description: Seconds to wait before recording starts. Default 1.

duration.description: Length of the recorded clip in seconds. Default 5.

aspectWarning.description: Editor-only guard for tools whose layout only suits a certain orientation (e.g. a portrait-only document). Shows a prominent warning beside the dimension controls when the current page aspect ratio (width ÷ height) falls outside [min, max]. Never affects — or appears in — the rendered/exported output.

min.description: Minimum acceptable aspect ratio (width ÷ height). Below this, the warning shows. Omit to leave the low end unbounded.

max.description: Maximum acceptable aspect ratio (width ÷ height). Above this, the warning shows. Omit to leave the high end unbounded.

message.description: The warning text shown to the editor.

inputs.description: Declares user-facing inputs. The host generates UI from this. The template references inputs by id.

capabilities.description: Declares what host capabilities this tool needs. The host disables the tool on shells that can't provide them (e.g. ffmpeg-using tool unavailable in web shell).

composes.description: Nested renders (tool composition). Each entry renders another tool's output to an embeddable asset, exposed to the template as an extra referenced via {{asset <id>}} (so a logic-less template can embed one tool's render inside another). Requires the 'compose' capability. Recursion is depth- and cycle-guarded by the host. Capped (maxItems) well under the render cache so a single tool's composes can't self-evict mid-render.

id.description: Name the composed asset is exposed under. Reference it in the template as {{asset <id>}}; guard with {{#if <id>}} since composition can fail gracefully.

tool.description: id of the tool to render.

inputs.description: Inputs passed to the composed tool, keyed by the child tool's input ids. String values are Handlebars, hydrated against THIS tool's own context (its input values + extras), so child inputs can bind to parent values, e.g. { "url": "{{url}}" }.

format.description: Render format for the composed output. Default: svg when the child supports it (vector embeds stay crisp in svg/pdf exports), else png.

width.description: Render width in px. Defaults to the composed tool's native width.

height.description: Render height in px. Defaults to the composed tool's native height.

hooks.description: Optional escape hatch. If present, the tool ships a hooks.js file that the host loads in a sandboxed context.

onFrame.description: The tool reacts to a live camera stream. When the shell provides host.media (a camera) the runtime drives this hook once per frame with { frame, model, host }, where frame is { width, height, data (RGBA), t }; the returned patch updates the render exactly like onInit/onInput. Pure progressive enhancement — the tool still runs as a still-image tool where no camera is available, so it must NOT be declared as a required 'camera' capability.

exportFile.description: The tool produces output via the transform path (file in → transformed file out) rather than rasterising the DOM. The hook reads the picked file's bytes (input.value.bytes) and returns a { bytes, mime, filename } record; the host wraps it in a Blob and delivers it via host.export.file (no watermark, no embedded provenance). Used by on-device utilities.

id.description: Referenced in the template as {{id}}. Also becomes a URL param.

bindToProfile.description: If set, pre-fills from the user profile field of this name (e.g. 'firstname', 'headshot'). User can override.

group.description: Optional grouping tag. The reserved value 'export' moves an input out of the sidebar into the export panel; the engine injects its own export-group toggles (transparentBg, convertPaths). Any other value is a free-form label for related controls.

showIf.description: Conditional visibility: the input renders in the sidebar only while every { otherInputId: value } pair here matches the current model (e.g. { "vectorize": true }).

palette.description: Asset ID of a palette asset to constrain choices. If omitted, free color picker.

swatchesOnly.description: Restrict to brand palette swatches only — hides the hex entry, native picker and alpha. Default false (full picker).

width.description: Export width this option implies, in `unit`. With `height`, selecting the option sets the export dimensions (so a 'size' select drives the printed page size). The shell applies it to the export bar; the user can still override.

height.description: Export height this option implies, in `unit`.

unit.description: Unit for this option's width/height. Defaults to 'mm' when width/height are present.

filter.description: Constraints passed to host.assets.query(). Limits the picker.

allowUpload.description: If true, picker offers user upload alongside library.

accept.description: Allowlist of accepted file types — MIME types and/or extensions (e.g. ["image/jpeg", "image/png", ".heic"]). Used as the file picker's `accept` attribute. Empty/omitted accepts any file. This is a UX hint, not a security boundary — the hook should still validate bytes.

maxSize.description: Maximum accepted file size in bytes. The host rejects larger files at pick time. Omit for no limit.

addMenu.description: Turns the '+ Add' button into a typed menu. Each option of the named discriminator sub-field becomes a menu entry; an entry already used is disabled unless its option sets repeatable:true. The discriminator is fixed at creation and shown as the block's label rather than an editable control.

field.description: id of the select sub-field whose options drive the menu.

labelledFields.description: Show a text label above each sub-field (the layout typed/addMenu blocks always use). Turn on for untyped blocks whose controls aren't self-evident, or that carry several optional fields.

fields.description: Sub-fields for each repeatable block entry. A field may declare a `type` (text|color|select|asset|number|boolean); text is the default. `showFor` limits a field to blocks whose discriminator value is listed; `showIf` limits it by sibling-field and/or top-level input values.

default.description: Value a newly-added block's field starts at.

help.description: Short hint shown under the sub-field (labelledFields blocks only).

showFor.description: Render this field only when the block's discriminator value is one of these.

showIf.description: Render this field only when the named values all match. Keys are looked up first among sibling fields of the same block, then among top-level input ids (e.g. { "vectorize": true, "tune": true }).

multilineFor.description: Render a text field as a multi-line textarea (rather than a single-line input) when the block's discriminator value is one of these.

rows.description: Visible rows for a multi-line text field (see multilineFor). Default 3.

options.description: Choices for a select sub-field. An option's repeatable:true lets the add-menu offer it more than once.

optionsFrom.description: Render this sub-field as a reference picker whose choices are the rows of another blocks input (or several). The stored value is each target row's derived id (slug of its key field, else its label, else an ordinal) — the same id a tool's hook resolves — so it replaces hand-typed ID cross-references with a dropdown. A stored value matching no current row is surfaced as an '(unknown)' option rather than silently dropped.

input.description: id of the blocks input to draw rows from (single-source form).

value.description: Source-row sub-field used as the stored id (slugged + de-duplicated to match the tool's resolution).

label.description: Source-row sub-field shown to the user (falls back to the derived id).

prefix.description: Ordinal id prefix for rows with neither key nor label.

sources.description: Several source inputs merged into one option list (e.g. cards AND layers). De-duplicated by value, first wins.

freeText.description: Render as a combobox (datalist) that also accepts a typed value not in the list — e.g. a kanban column invented on the spot.

excludeSelf.description: Omit the current row from its own options (a card can't be its own parent).

excludeDescendants.description: Omit the current row's descendants (requires the input's `nesting`) so a node can't reparent into its own subtree.

emptyLabel.description: Label for the blank/none option (default '— none —').

nesting.description: Treat this blocks array as an editable TREE: the sidebar renders it as an indented outline and the header drag lets a card be dropped above, below, or INSIDE another, updating the dragged card's parent reference (its whole subtree travels with it). The data stays a flat reference-by-id array — only the presentation is tree-shaped — so it serialises and renders exactly as before.

parentField.description: Sub-field holding each row's parent reference (the id of another row).

keyField.description: Sub-field used as a row's id (what parentField points at).

labelField.description: Fallback id source when keyField is blank, and the human label for indentation.

prefix.description: Ordinal id prefix for rows with neither key nor label.

activeWhen.description: Only treat the input as a tree when these top-level input values match (an array value matches by membership), e.g. { "diagramType": ["org", "mindmap"] }. Omit to always nest.

dropToAdd.description: Turns the blocks list into a drop zone: dragging or selecting several image files at once creates one block per file, each with the file uploaded into the named asset sub-field. Every other field of a new block starts at its declared default.

field.description: id of the block's `asset` sub-field that each dropped file is uploaded into.

accept.description: MIME accept filter for dropped files (default "image/*"). A trailing /* matches a whole type group.

canvas.description: Marks this blocks array as a free-form WYSIWYG canvas of positioned 'boxes' (requires render.layout:'editor'). The web shell mounts a direct-manipulation overlay — select, drag, resize, rotate, re-stack (z-order = array order, later rows paint on top), align and distribute — that reads and writes this flat array through the normal input path. The fields named below give the overlay the geometry to manipulate; every field remains an ordinary block sub-field, so the layout stays flat, URL-expressible, and renders identically headless (CLI/URL) with no overlay. Field ORDER in `fields` is the compact-URL wire format — never reorder or remove once shipped.

idField.description: Sub-field holding each box's stable id (used to track selection across re-stacking). Default 'id'.

xField.description: Number sub-field: box left, in canvas (native render) pixels. Default 'x'.

yField.description: Number sub-field: box top, in canvas pixels. Default 'y'.

wField.description: Number sub-field: box width, in canvas pixels. Default 'w'.

hField.description: Number sub-field: box height, in canvas pixels. Default 'h'.

rotationField.description: Number sub-field: clockwise rotation in degrees about the box centre. Default 'rot'.

radiusField.description: Number sub-field: corner radius in px (used when shape is rounded).

fillField.description: Color sub-field: box background fill (empty = transparent).

imageField.description: Asset sub-field: image drawn inside the box.

fitField.description: Select sub-field: how the image fills the box (object-fit: cover/contain/fill).

blendField.description: Select sub-field: CSS mix-blend-mode for the whole box (faithful in raster export; flattened in vector).

textField.description: Text sub-field: text drawn inside the box.

fontSizeField.description: Number sub-field: text size in px.

minSize.description: Smallest box width/height the overlay will create or resize to, in canvas px. Default 8.

addKinds.description: The kinds of box the 'add' tool offers. Each entry seeds a new box's fields (id excluded — the shell assigns it).

id.description: Kind id (also the default select value of the shape/kind field if present).

icon.description: Optional inline SVG or glyph for the menu entry.

seed.description: Field values a newly-added box of this kind starts at (merged over the block's declared defaults).

fields.description: Numeric sub-fields, each a labelled Figma-style scrubber. Stored as one compound value { fieldId: number } and rendered as a single control / column.

symbol.description: Single character shown inside the field as its scrub handle (e.g. "+" for zoom). Defaults to the first letter of the label.
